column5: Chapter Title (Mandatory)
column7: Question ID (Mandatory and Unique, for example: 1.1, 1.2, Etc)
column10: Answer Type (Mandatory, one of the following options: 1 for Open Answer, 2 for Dropdown - Single Select, 3 for Dropdown - Single Select - Open answer, 4 for Date, 5 for Dropdown - Multiple Select, 6 for Dropdown - Multiple Select - Open answer)
column11: Dropdown Options (Mandatory if column G is Dropdown. List options for the dropdown separated by a pipe character, for example: Yes|No|Undefined)
column12: DropDown Warnings (Optional, for every option on column H you can define a message, for example: Great|Oh|Think twice)
column13: Dropdown Scoring (Mandatory, set this to 1 if you want to enable scoring for this question. Otherwise set to 0)
column14: Dropdown Option Score (Mandatory if J is set to 1, set the score of the question as an integer positive number, for example 10)
column15: Dropdown Option Score Multiplier (Mandatory if J is set to 1. For every option of the dropdown in column H define an integer positive number that will be multiplied by the value on column K, for example: 1|2|10)
column16: Dropdown Option Show another Question (Optional and only possible if column G is dropdown.For every option in column H you can define one or more question IDs from column D to be shown when the option is selected. For example: 1.2 1.6||6.7 would display questions 1.2 and 1.6 if the first option is selected, none if the second is selected and 6.7 if the third option is selected)
column17: Question Comments & Attachments (Mandatory, select 0 if you do not want to show this option or 1 if you want to let users provide comments and attachments for each question)

column3: ### Definition of Information Security Information security is the preservation of the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information, regardless of its form. ### Information Security Objectives The organization establishes information security objectives to: - Protect information assets against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or loss. - Maintain availability of systems and services supporting critical business processes. - Manage information security risks in line with the Risk Management Policy. - Maintain customer, regulatory, and stakeholder trust. ### Information Security Principles All information security activities shall be guided by the following principles: - Information security supports business objectives. - Risk-based decision making is applied. - Least privilege and need-to-know access is enforced. - Security controls are proportionate to risk. - Information security is a shared responsibility. ### Compliance and Applicable Requirements The organization commits to satisfying applicable: - Legal and regulatory requirements - Contractual obligations - Information security requirements agreed with customers and suppliers ### Continual Improvement The organization commits to the continual improvement of the Information Security Management System (ISMS) through: - Risk assessment and treatment - Monitoring and measurement - Audits and reviews - Management oversight ### Roles and Responsibilities Information security roles and responsibilities are defined and governed through the Information Security Roles and Responsibilities Policy. ### Exceptions and Exemptions - Exceptions to this policy or supporting information security policies must be formally requested. - Exceptions must be risk-assessed, approved, documented, and time-bound. - Approved exceptions must be reviewed periodically. ---
column5: Does this policy statement apply to your company: Protect information assets against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or loss.
